stories.headline: Aave Stares Down $195M Bad Debt After Kelp Hacker Borrows Against Stolen rsETH
stories.tldr: Aave is quantifying how much bad debt it now holds after the Kelp DAO exploiter used stolen rsETH as collateral to borrow roughly $195 million in ETH.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: If Aave absorbs the loss, its safety module could be tapped for the first time in years — a live stress test for DeFi's largest lender.
the_details: Hacker borrowed about $195M in ETH on Aave using drained rsETH from Kelp's $292M cross-chain bridge exploit.
Aave net deposits fell roughly $6.2B (-23%) over the weekend; the AAVE token dropped about 18% on Sunday.
Total DeFi TVL sank from $26.4B on April 18 to near $20B by Sunday morning ET as contagion spread.

stories.headline: DeFi On Pace for Worst Hack Year Ever: 47 Exploits, Up 68% YoY
stories.tldr: DeFi has logged 47 security incidents through mid-April 2026 — a 68% jump over the same period in 2025 — putting it on track for its worst year for hacks.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Insurance premiums, audit demand, and regulatory pressure all ratchet up when the sector bleeds this consistently; capital follows trust, not tokenomics.
the_details: 47 DeFi hacks recorded in the first 4.5 months of 2026 versus 28 over the same period in 2025.
Combined haul topped $450M in the last two weeks alone, led by Kelp ($292M) and Drift ($285M).
Ledger's CTO called 2026 'the worst year in terms of hacks' for DeFi on record.

stories.headline: SEC Chair Atkins Puts Crypto Atop 2026 Priorities in Debut Agency Podcast
stories.tldr: SEC Chair Paul Atkins, joined by Commissioners Uyeda and Peirce, flagged digital assets as a top 2026 priority on the agency's inaugural internal podcast.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Explicit chair-level signaling is the clearest tailwind DeFi and tokenization builders have gotten from the SEC in years.
the_details: Atkins: 'In one area now that is really top on our list... is the whole digital asset area, crypto assets.'
The inaugural Material Matters episode aired April 16, titled 'Commissioners Set the Course: 2026 Priorities.'
Follows April 13 staff guidance letting DEX frontend 'Covered User Interface Providers' operate without broker-dealer registration.

stories.headline: CoinDesk 20 Tanks 3.6% as AAVE Craters 22.9% in Weekend Contagion Bloodbath
stories.tldr: The CoinDesk 20 index fell 3.6% since Friday's close with none of its 20 assets positive, led by AAVE's 22.9% weekend collapse.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A full-index red print plus a blue-chip DeFi name losing nearly a quarter of its value tells you the Kelp hit is being priced as systemic, not isolated.
the_details: CoinDesk 20 traded at 2085.29 on Monday, down 3.6% from Friday's 4pm ET close.
AAVE -22.9% and ICP -7.9% were the biggest laggards; BNB -2.3% and BTC -2.5% held up best.
The CMC Altcoin Season Index sits at 34/100 — squarely in Bitcoin-Season territory.

stories.headline: Crypto Billionaire Offers Direct Talks to North Korea-Linked Kelp Hacker
stories.tldr: A prominent crypto billionaire has publicly proposed direct negotiations with the attacker behind the $290M Kelp exploit, now suspected of DPRK ties.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Public hacker-negotiation offers are becoming a 2026 playbook — recovering funds beats chasing slow state-level attribution.
the_details: Offer follows Drift's $285M exploit earlier in April, also attributed to DPRK-linked actors.
Kelp hack drained about 116,500 rsETH — 18% of supply — via a misconfigured LayerZero cross-chain setup.
DPRK-linked crypto theft across April now exceeds $575M combined between Drift and Kelp.
